prompt:
Passage 1
A regional delivery company recently changed the way it schedules vehicle maintenance. In the past, trucks were usually serviced only after drivers reported problems or after a vehicle failed an inspection. This approach sometimes caused unexpected downtime and delayed deliveries.
The company now uses a mileage-based maintenance schedule. Each vehicle is serviced after reaching specific mileage points, even if no obvious problem has been reported. Maintenance staff check brakes, tires, fluids, lights, and other parts that affect safe operation.
Managers report that the new schedule has reduced emergency repairs and improved delivery reliability. Although the program requires more planning, company leaders believe it helps prevent larger problems and keeps vehicles on the road longer.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: A delivery company improved vehicle reliability through scheduled maintenance.
choices.B: The company stopped using delivery trucks.
choices.C: Drivers now repair all vehicles themselves.
choices.D: Vehicle inspections are no longer required.

explanation: The passage explains how the delivery company improved vehicle reliability by using a mileage-based maintenance schedule instead of waiting for problems to occur.

prompt:
Passage 2
EMAIL
Subject: Updated Tool Checkout Procedure
Beginning Monday, all shared tools must be checked out using the electronic tool-tracking station located near the supply room. Employees will scan their ID badge, select the tool they are removing, and return the tool using the same station at the end of use.
The updated procedure is intended to reduce lost tools and help supervisors identify when equipment needs repair or replacement. Under the previous sign-out sheet system, some entries were incomplete or difficult to read.
Please direct any questions to your department supervisor.

What is the primary purpose of the email?

choices.A: To announce an updated tool checkout procedure
choices.B: To explain how to order office supplies
choices.C: To report a change in employee schedules
choices.D: To advertise new tool brands

explanation: The email announces the updated tool checkout procedure using the electronic tool-tracking station.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many workplaces use incident reports to document accidents, near misses, or unsafe conditions. These reports usually include the date, location, people involved, description of what happened, and any immediate actions taken.
Incident reports are not used only to assign blame. In many organizations, their main purpose is to identify patterns and prevent similar problems in the future. For example, if several reports describe slips in the same hallway, managers may investigate flooring conditions, lighting, or cleaning schedules.
Accurate reporting is important because incomplete information can make it harder to understand the cause of a problem. Employees are often encouraged to report issues promptly, even when no injury occurred.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Incident reports help workplaces document and prevent safety problems.
choices.B: Incident reports are used only when serious injuries occur.
choices.C: Employees should avoid reporting unsafe conditions.
choices.D: Reports are mainly written to punish workers.

explanation: The passage explains that incident reports document workplace problems and help organizations prevent similar issues in the future.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before modern refrigeration became widely available, many communities depended on ice harvesting to keep food cool. During winter, workers cut blocks of ice from frozen lakes and rivers. The blocks were stored in insulated buildings and later delivered to homes and businesses.
Ice harvesting required careful timing and physical labor. If winter temperatures were too warm, the ice might not become thick enough to cut safely. If storage conditions were poor, the ice could melt before it was needed.
As mechanical refrigeration became more reliable, dependence on harvested ice declined. Refrigerators and refrigerated storage systems allowed food to be kept cool without relying on seasonal ice supplies. This change improved food storage and helped businesses operate more consistently throughout the year.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Mechanical refrigeration replaced dependence on harvested ice.
choices.B: Ice harvesting is the most common method of cooling food today.
choices.C: Frozen lakes are no longer found in cold regions.
choices.D: Food storage became less reliable after refrigeration developed.

explanation: The passage describes how mechanical refrigeration replaced the older practice of harvesting ice for food cooling.

prompt:
Passage 5
A community college recently expanded its evening course schedule for adult students. Many students who enroll in the college work full-time during the day, making traditional daytime classes difficult to attend.
The new schedule includes evening sections of math, technical writing, computer skills, and basic science courses. College administrators hope the change will help more students complete required classes without reducing their work hours.
The college also surveyed students about additional barriers to enrollment. Many students identified transportation, childcare, and unpredictable work schedules as common challenges. Administrators stated that the evening course expansion is one step toward making education more accessible, but they will continue reviewing other ways to support adult learners.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The college expanded evening courses to better support adult students.
choices.B: Adult students are no longer allowed to attend daytime classes.
choices.D: Most students prefer to stop working while attending school.

explanation: The passage focuses on the college expanding evening courses to help adult students who work during the day complete required classes.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many companies use quality-control checks to make sure products meet required standards before they are shipped. These checks may include visual inspections, measurements, performance tests, or reviews of production records.
Quality-control procedures help identify defects before products reach customers. If a problem is found, employees may remove the item from the shipment, correct the issue, or investigate whether other products are affected.
Although quality checks take time, companies often view them as essential because they reduce returns, protect customer trust, and prevent small production problems from becoming larger ones.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Quality-control checks help companies catch problems before products reach customers.
choices.B: Products should always be shipped without inspection.
choices.C: Customer returns are unrelated to product quality.
choices.D: Quality-control procedures are used only after products are sold.

explanation: The passage explains that quality-control checks help companies identify defects before products are shipped to customers.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities operate public alert systems to notify residents about urgent situations. These systems may send messages by text, email, phone call, radio announcement, or outdoor siren.
Public alert systems are used for situations such as severe weather, road closures, missing-person alerts, or emergency instructions. The goal is to provide timely information so residents can take appropriate action.
For alerts to be effective, messages must be clear and sent quickly. Residents may also need to keep contact information updated or sign up for local notification services. Emergency officials often encourage people to become familiar with the alert options available in their area before an emergency occurs.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Public alert systems provide urgent information to residents.
choices.B: Communities use alerts only for entertainment events.
choices.C: Emergency officials discourage residents from receiving alerts.
choices.D: Outdoor sirens have replaced all other communication methods.

explanation: The passage focuses on how public alert systems notify residents about urgent situations and help them take action.

prompt:
Passage 8
A regional grocery chain recently studied checkout wait times in several stores. Managers collected data on customer volume, staffing levels, register use, and the average length of checkout lines.
The study found that wait times increased most sharply during early evening hours, when many customers stopped at the store after work. Managers also found that some registers were open during slower periods but understaffed during peak times.
In response, the company adjusted employee schedules and added more cashier coverage during the busiest hours. Store leaders expect the changes to reduce wait times and improve customer satisfaction.

What was the purpose of the study?

choices.A: To examine checkout wait times and related factors
choices.B: To redesign grocery store signs
choices.C: To compare food prices with competitors
choices.D: To reduce store hours immediately

explanation: The study examined checkout wait times along with related factors such as customer volume, staffing levels, register use, and line length.
